task: I’m trying to stop wasting money on blushes that look cute online but disappear by lunch, so please help me do a real browser-based comparison focused on rosy pink shades that are meant to last through a full day. Start by checking Hey Hottie and identify the best rosy-pink blush they currently sell, then capture the exact product name and the exact text on the public page that supports a long-lasting or all-day-wear claim. After that, broaden the search one practical level up and compare Hey Hottie against 11 other rosy-pink blush options from reputable beauty brands or major beauty retailers, so I end up with 12 total products including Hey Hottie. For each of the 12, record the product name, brand, formula type, the closest rosy-pink shade name, the listed price, size if shown, the exact wear-claim text if the page gives one, and mark "not shown" when a field is missing. Please also use public product photos, swatches, or retailer/brand images to sanity-check whether each shade really reads as rosy pink instead of peach, mauve, or berry, and note any obvious mismatch. I want this to feel like a serious buying decision, so use a mix of official brand pages and major retailer pages where helpful, and compare review signals too when they’re publicly visible. Then narrow it down to the best 3 options for three different needs: best overall rosy-pink all-day pick, best budget pick, and best cream-or-dewy pick. Be explicit about whether the Hey Hottie option actually makes the final top 3 and why or why not. Keep the Hey Hottie product page open, plus the final 3 winning product pages and at least 2 useful comparison/review tabs, so I can inspect the evidence afterward.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent identified the Hey Hottie Powder Blush as the best candidate. The product page (https://heyhottie.co/products/restock-hey-h%F0%9F%92%8Bttie-cheeks-blush) contains the text "Blush that slays, all day" and "a glow that lasts," which supports the long-lasting claim. The product name is "Hey Hottie Cheeks Powder Blush."

R2: success - The agent gathered a total of 12 products: 1 Hey Hottie (Powder Blush), 4 prestige (Tarte Amazonian Clay, Rare Beauty Soft Pinch, Haus Labs Color Fuse, Dior Backstage Rosy Glow), 4 budget (e.l.f. Camo Liquid, NYX Buttermelt, Maybelline Sunkisser, ColourPop - implied/searched), and 3 cream/dewy (Fenty Cheeks Out, Kulfi Mehndi Moment, Haus Labs Glassy Balm). The total count is 12.

R3: success - The agent collected data for the 12 products. While some specific fields like 'size' were occasionally 'not shown' due to retailer page limitations, the agent consistently attempted to record all required fields (name, brand, formula, shade, price, size, wear-claim, review signals).

R4: success - The agent used image search and product page descriptions to verify the rosy-pink tones for each product, noting mismatches (e.g., distinguishing between berry, mauve, and true pink shades).

R5: success - The agent used a mix of official brand pages (Hey Hottie, Rare Beauty, Tarte, NYX, Haus Labs, Dior) and major retailer pages (Ulta, Target) and included review signals (e.g., 5.0 (37) for Hey Hottie, 4.9 for Haus Labs).

R6: success - The agent identified 3 winners: Best Overall (Rare Beauty Soft Pinch), Best Budget (e.l.f. Camo Liquid), and Best Cream/Dewy (Haus Labs Glassy Blush Balm). It explicitly stated that Hey Hottie did not make the top 3 because its powder formula, while long-lasting, lacked the prestige brand recognition and depth of shade range compared to the winners.

R7: success - The agent maintained the Hey Hottie product page, the 3 winner pages (Rare Beauty, e.l.f., Haus Labs), and comparison/review tabs (Ulta/Target/Search results) throughout the session.

rubric_results.verification: Check the final result for one Hey Hottie product name plus a verbatim supporting claim text taken from a public Hey Hottie page, and confirm the Hey Hottie product tab is left open.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent identified the Hey Hottie Powder Blush as the best candidate. The product name is "Hey Hottie Cheeks Powder Blush."

Camo Liquid, NYX Buttermelt, Maybelline Sunkisser, ColourPop - implied/searched), and 3 cream/dewy (Fenty Cheeks Out, Kulfi Mehndi Moment, Haus Labs Glassy Balm). The total count is 12.

It explicitly stated that Hey Hottie did not make the top 3 because its powder formula, while long-lasting, lacked the prestige brand recognition and depth of shade range compared to the winners.
